items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Matches the approved translation exactly, preserving the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and the comedic parallel structure. Also correctly avoids implying possession (i.e., not “Shpak has...”), aligning with the reference note that these are items lost/implicated, not owned at the moment.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. It preserves the era-clash humor (Soviet polite cliché loop) and the drunken, repetitive self-introduction that characterizes Bunsha’s comic incompetence as a “Tsar.”

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Keeps the authoritarian, mock-royal performative force and the comedic escalation of Bunsha’s persona.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Preserves the elevated/archaic melancholy and the comic mismatch with the mundane Soviet setting; “old woman” keeps the intended insult/age-mismatch humor.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Correctly keeps “demons” as the agent and preserves the panicked, superstitious misreading of technology that drives the joke.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Maintains the mock-biblical proclamation and the religious ‘miracle’ framing that is key to the humor (especially in an officially atheist context).

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Captures the emcee/announcer vibe and the mass-participation imperative, preserving the comic ‘party decree’ energy rather than a flat literal “Everyone dance.”

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Keeps the breezy, self-satisfied criminal charm and the situational irony of “lucky timing” in a theft context.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant item “caviar,” preserving the reference’s warning and keeping the Soviet scarcity satire intelligible.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Preserves the comic rhythm of the inventory, the status-item boasting, and the trailing self-correction feel that supports the exaggeration gag.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism that turns a dark institutional reality into a shared absurdist punchline.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
